text: The robotic arms of the da Vinci system are docked, and the procedure begins with the detachment of the colopiploid ligament and the opening of the lesser sac. The surgeon then dissects the splenocolic ligament, successfully mobilizing the splenic flexure.

The surgeon separates the descending mesocolon from Toldt's fascia, dividing the inferior mesenteric vein at its origin. The left colonic artery is divided at its root, preserving the origins of the inferior mesenteric artery and sigmoid artery. A mechanical stapler is used to dissect both the descending and transverse colon. Following the opening of the left paracolic gutter, the descending colon is mobilized.

The surgeon performs an intracorporeal side-to-side isoperistaltic anastomosis, followed by interotomy closure using a double-layer barb-running suture. The specimen is extracted through a mini-phanacin incision.
